title: Which Antigravity surface should I use? 2.0 vs CLI vs IDE vs SDK
summary: Google Antigravity is an agent-first software development platform that helps developers build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents connected to code editors, terminals, and browsers. It offers multiple interfaces including a standalone 2.0 desktop app, IDE, CLI, and SDK to support different agentic workflows.
writeup: The video compares the four surfaces of Google Antigravity, an agent-first software development platform for building, orchestrating, and monitoring AI agents connected to code editors, terminals, and browsers. It walks through the standalone 2.0 desktop app, the IDE, the CLI, and the SDK, mapping each to different agentic workflows. The takeaway is a framework for matching a surface to the task at hand rather than defaulting to one entry point.
